source_units.text_raw:
The RM zone is intended to a range of medium density dwelling types that allow for gentle densification in suitable locations with developments of up to three storeys and one dwelling unit per 115 square metres of lot area.
Rezonings are not permitted in the RM Zone except:
Rezoning from RM to RH may be considered where the lot fronts on a Collector Road and the policies of the Official Plan can be demonstrated.
The following uses are permitted in the RM Zone:
Townhouse Dwelling
Mini-home Dwelling
Modular Dwelling
Multi-unit Dwelling
Lodging House
Seniors Housing
Tourist Accommodation
Government Offices
Library
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
(a) Lot Area min. 230m2 multi-unit dwelling, 165m2 for a semi detached dwelling or townhouse
(b) Frontage min. 8 m for semi detached dwelling, 10 m for duplex dwelling, 5 m for townhouse 20 m for multi-unit or cluster townhouse condos
(c) Front Yard Setback min. 6 m
(d) Flankage Yard Setback min. 6 m
(e) Side Yard Setback min. 0 m shared*, 2 m for duplex or semi detached dwelling, 3 m for end townhouse or cluster, 6 m for multi- unit dwelling
(f) Rear Yard Setback min. 6 m
(g) Coverage max. 50%, 60% if half the parking is located underground
* 'shared' includes the shared walls of a townhouse or semi.
Cluster housing is permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
(a) Coverage max. 60%
(b) Footprint / building max. 90 m2
(c) Density min. one unit per 115 m2 of lot area
(d) Units max. 8 units per cluster
(e) Separation between units min. 3 m
(f) Central garden area min. 25 m2 / unit
One rear-yard or side-yard Accessory Dwelling Units (ADU's) per lot may be permitted if the conditions in Sections 4.6 can be met.
One Attached ADUs may be permitted if the conditions in Section 4.5 can be met.
Flaglots are permitted with no less than 4 m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 11.3.2 for both the flaglot and main lot.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.8 requirements are met.
Home daycare uses are permitted if Section 4.9 requirements are met.
Neighbourhood local commercial is permitted in the RM Zone along any collector or arterial road as identified in Schedule C up to a gross floor area of 120 m2 and by meeting the following requirements:
Three dedicated off-street automobile parking spaces and 2 Class B biking parking spaces; and
A minimum 1.5 m wide hard surface sidewalk linking the front entry of the store with the parking area.
A landscape buffer 2 m wide shall be constructed which includes an opaque fence no less than 1.8 m high along all properties that directly abut the commercial use or any associated parking.
One primary building per lot is permitted with the exception of cluster housing.
The maximum building height for the RM zone is 3 storeys.
Any garage (attached or detached) shall not project any closer to the streetline than the longest wall of the attached main dwelling that faces the same streetline.
Garage doors shall not exceed 50% of the primary building frontage on any streetline to a maximum of 7 m in total.
Developers are encouraged to use materials and styles consistent with existing neighbourhood character.
All street-facing facades shall provide at least one horizontal or vertical facade articulation of no less than 150 mm for no less than 30% of the facade area.
Townhouse clusters shall not exceed 6 consecutive units.
Townhouses may be oriented perpendicular to the street as long as
The building has frontage on a street and meets the fire code requirements.
Provides a connected walkway from each door to the street
Parking is in the rear or side yard
The building is located on one lot such that units are rental or condominium
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
All driveways shall be hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete or unit pavers. Loose gravel or soil is not permitted.
Parking in the front yard is only permitted on a hard surfaced driveways or parking spots with no more than 1 car per driveway.
No more than 40% of the front yard area can be driveway or parking spaces.
A minimum 1.5 m wide hard surfaced pathway shall link the driveway to the front door of every ground floor unit.
No less than 1 (min. 2 m high) tree shall be planted on each lot or 0.5 trees per unit, whichever is greater. Decimals shall be rounded up.
No less than 3 m2 of shrub beds shall be planted per groundfloor unit consisting of shrubs, annuals or perennials.
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
Each grade-related unit shall provide no less than 4 m2 of private outdoor amenity space (patio, balcony, rooftop deck, or small yard) to ensure resident comfort and privacy.
